chunk_summary: Case-based management of recurrent pneumonia in an 8-year-old with spastic quadriplegic CP, focusing on feeding safety, nutrition, and interim tube feeding strategies to address aspiration risk and prolonged mealtimes.
learning_objectives: Identify high-risk feeding behaviors and their impact on respiratory health in children with neurodisability.
Develop an interim feeding plan addressing swallow safety, nutrition, and hydration in aspiration-prone patients.
Understand the role of NGT bridge trials and gastrostomy in managing feeding-related complications.
Evaluate the trade-offs of thickened fluids in managing aspiration risk.
key_facts: The patient experienced three antibiotic-treated pneumonias in 12 months, indicating high aspiration risk.
Meals lasted 75–90 minutes, with weight plateauing for 8 months, highlighting poor feeding efficiency.
Interim plan includes IDDSI-tested thickened liquids, 90–90–90 positioning, reduced bolus size, and meal duration capped at 30 minutes.
NGT bridge trials (2–4 weeks) are recommended to assess benefits before gastrostomy decisions.
Thickened fluids may reduce coughing but can worsen thirst, constipation, and medication delivery.
reference_quote: ‘Thickened fluids may reduce visible coughing but can worsen thirst, constipation, and medication delivery; state these trade-offs during consent and monitor within 1–2 weeks.’ (Source: https://espghan.info/files/Recommendations-for-...)
